chunks.text: ORDER SHEET THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT LARKANO Cr. Bail Appln. No. S-76 of 2025 Date Orders with signature of Judge 1. For orders on M.A No.792/2025 (U/A) 2. For orders on office objection “A”. 3. For orders on M.A No.793/2025 (E/A) 4. For hearing of bail application. 5. For orders on M.A No.792/2025. 12-02-2025 Mr. Fada Hussain Khan, advocate for the applicant a/w applicant.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-. 1. Urgency application is allowed. 2 to 5. Through this application, the applicant has sought for protective bail in crime No.886/2024, registered at PS Shah Latif Malir, for the offence u/s 302, 324, 109, 34 PPC. It is contended by the learned counsel for the applicant that the applicant is innocent and he has falsely been involved in this case by the complainant due to malafide intention and ulterior motives; that no overt act has been assigned against the applicant/accused. Learned counsel further submits that applicant is redy to surrender before the trial court but due to issuance of N.B.Ws he apprehends imminent arrest at the hands of police, hence the applicant is unable to approach the concerned court, therefore, he needs protective bail enabling him to approach the learned trial court. He, therefore, prays for grant of protective bail. Be that as it may, without dilating upon the merits of the case applicant is admitted to protective bail for seven days from the date of this order, subject to his furnishing a solvent surety in the sum of Rs.100,000/- (Rupees one hundred thousand) and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the Additional Registrar of this court. This order shall cease to have effect on expiry of period of seven days or on the date when the applicant surrenders himself before the concerned court, whichever is earlier.

Be that as it may, without dilating upon the merits of the case applicant is admitted to protective bail for seven days from the date of this order, subject to his furnishing a solvent surety in the sum of Rs.100,000/- (Rupees one hundred thousand) and P.R bond in the like amount to the satisfaction of the Additional Registrar of this court. This order shall cease to have effect on expiry of period of seven days or on the date when the applicant surrenders himself before the concerned court, whichever is earlier. The bail application stands disposed of in the above terms. Judge
